title: ST / Spell Checker / Add Spanish Lexicon

details.problem: As a Spanish translator, I type almost exclusively in Spanish. And Epic underlines every single word that I type as being misspelled. Thankfully, Epic finally supports choosing the spell check language per user (XDS 661862). But Epic doesn't provide a Spanish lexicon!
details.ideaForSolution: Please provide a Spanish lexicon.
details.whyNow: Supporting Spanish Spell Check (and other common secondary languages) is the logical next step after XDS 661862, which shipped with the August 2025 Release. It is now reasonable for users to expect that Epic supports spell check for at least the most common secondary languages. And Spanish is by far the most common secondary languages in the US.
details.smartToolsFit: "Create tools to improve efficiency of documentation". With erroneous underlining of typed Spanish content, it hinders rather than helps users to write good content. Users may instead learn to ignore the underline or disable that feature.
details.epicFit: "Solving existing pain points, such as ideas frequently reported during immersion, submitted through ideas.epic.com, or from other forms of feedback." Notice that this QAN already has 7 SLGs attached.
details.maintenance: Unsure. On the initial release, we'll need to do some extensive testing to verify that the lexicon is decent. But after that, we'll be able to mostly leave it alone, making only small tweaks as customers report spurious or missing words.

title: ST / EpicCommand Link / Provide A Better Way For User To Insert EpicCommand Link

details.problem: As a user of EpicCommand links, I want to enter parameters in a clear and structured way, so that I don't have to remember or worry about delimiters, formatting rules, or accidentally entering invalid values.
details.ideaForSolution: As a user creating an EpicCommand link, I can select a parameter type (e.g., patient ID, record ID, etc.) and enter its value in a dedicated field with built-in validation, so that I can easily construct links without worrying about delimiters, formatting rules, or invalid inputs.
details.whyNow: Saves build time for EpicCommand link. Work is being done in this area right now, but it is fairly bare bones without this proposed development. We are actively recommending organizations transition from EpicAct links to EpicCommand links. The smoother and more intuitive the EpicCommand link experience becomes, the easier it will be for organizations to adopt it and fully move away from EpicAct links. By improving usability and reducing errors now, we remove barriers to adoption and accelerate the retirement of outdated functionality.
details.success: Average users can build an EpicCommand link without any confusion.

title: AC / ALLOW MARKING USER SMARTPHRASES AS PERSONAL/SENSITIVE TO HIDE FROM OTHER EDITORS

details.problem: Users might create a SmartPhrase for a signature that doesn't make sense for other people to have access to or as a personal reference that isn't meant for others to use. If other people use their private SmartPhrases, this can make them feel like their privacy has been violated or annoyed that they can't control their personal smartphrases.
details.ideaForSolution: Allow end users the choice to hide their SmartPhrases that aren't appropriate for others to use, preventing other users from searching or sharing SmartPhrases without the user's permission. This will make end users feel like they have control over their SmartPhrases.
details.characteristics: Create a button in the user SmartPhrase editor that allows it to only be searchable or viewable by editors so SmartPhrases that aren't meant to be shared don't have to be. There could also be an HDF setting to allow organizations to turn this feature off if they don't think SmartPhrases should ever be private. Private SmartPhrases should able to be changed back to public SmartPhrases at any point and vice versa, and allow it to be shared limitedly if users are added as editors so it can be shared if the owner gives permission.
details.whyNow: It was recently marked as a potential SPRE by a customer because they saw it as such a concern and it is regularly requested in ideas.epic.com.
details.smartToolsFit: It will improve effectiveness because users already have the signature SmartPhrases but it will increase trust and feelings of security if they know that their personal information can't be taken by others.
details.epicFit: This fits ST priorities because it would solve long standing existing pain points.
details.success: SmartPhrases can be made private.

title: AC / SMARTTEXTBOX / FIXED-WIDTH TABLES: CAN THESE SUPPORT RESIZING?

details.problem: While editing notes in the sidebar, tables can be too wide to fully display, causing users to need to horizontally scroll. Alternately, tables could be narrower than available space, causing extra vertical scrolling. This lack of table sizing can make text harder to review at a glance, and cause physicians to need to manually format tables to fit available space, or the page.
details.ideaForSolution: Because of the risk/change in behavior having tables automatically resize has been (we attempted this with the web migration) I think we should do a limited/opt-in for resize. This might be something that's enabled for table SmartLinks outside of page mode, or some other subset that would be a usability win, but be limited in scope such that we are not causing downstream effects in customer build.
details.success: Providers will find that tables are in view and have a seamless experience viewing tables. If we have metrics on table resizing, or horizontal scrolling, a decrease in those.

title: AC/SMARTTOOL ADMIN/ABILITY TO MAP OR LINK A SMARTPHRASE TO A SMARTDATA ELEMENT

details.problem: Analysts add SDEs to SmartTexts and SmartLists so compliance officers can track metrics and reporting. Since SmartPhrases can't have SDEs, analysts are having to build workarounds with SmartTexts in SmartLinks, requiring extra time and awkward workflows.
details.ideaForSolution: Allow analysts to add SDEs to system SmartPhrases in the SmartPhrase editor so SmartPhrases will add SDEs when end users add those SmartPhrases to their notes.
details.characteristics: SDEs are applied when the SmartPhrase is used, removed if the SmartPhrase text is removed, and can be used to track usage for reporting.
details.whyNow: It's still being requested regularly.
details.smartToolsFit: Make it easy to do the right thing.
details.epicFit: This fits ST priorities because it would solve long standing existing pain points.

title: PREVENT PARTS OF A NOTE TEMPLATE FROM BEING DELETED

details.problem: Organizational leads and compliance officers often have required SmartLists or documentation that they add for legal reasons. These sections must be filled out but end users can delete them from any note and sign without completing the documentation.
details.ideaForSolution: Add a SmartLink that could be used similarly to a sectioning SmartLink but nothing in that section can be deleted in the note. Alternatively, provide a warn-on-delete SmartLink that shows a confirmation/warning when deletion is attempted.
details.characteristics: Allow sections of a SmartText to be required in a note in a way that makes deleting it difficult.
details.smartToolsFit: This would be a part of making it easy for users to do the right thing so that required documentation is actually filled out.
details.epicFit: This fits ST priorities because it would solve long standing existing pain points.
details.success: Parts of a template can be restricted from being deleted when added to a STB.

title: EMC2: SMARTLISTS NOT REFRESHABLE

details.problem: SmartLists that contain SmartTexts (or nested SmartLinks that resolve to SmartTexts) often become non-reselectable/ non-refreshable once selected; this causes downstream SDE/HLV and reporting issues and forces users to delete and re-add content to change selections.
details.ideaForSolution: Provide a way for SmartList selections with embedded SmartTools to remain reselectable, perhaps via a builder-controlled setting or logic determining when a selection should remain refreshable.
details.characteristics: Provide a way for picking SmartList selections with embedded SmartTools and have the SmartList stay reselectable; consider a new setting in the SmartList whether selections should stay reselectable when picked.
details.whyNow: Common pain point for EMC2 and end user workflows.
details.epicFit: Solving existing pain points, such as ideas frequently reported during immersion, submitted through ideas.epic.com, or from other forms of feedback.

title: ST / Pitch / Only three asterisks should be a hardstop for unreoslved SmartTools, 4+ shouldn't block signing notes

details.problem: As an end user, I can be blocked from signing my note because content coming in over interfaces can have longer strings of asterisks (e.g., LabCorp) that get interpreted as wildcards when pulled into notes, preventing signing or creating confusion.
details.ideaForSolution: Make the system treat exactly three asterisks as a wildcard but ignore longer strings of asterisks, or otherwise avoid treating decorative long asterisk strings from interfaces as wildcards.
details.characteristics: Update validation to check for exactly three asterisks in a row to be treated as a wildcard; four or more would not be a wildcard.
details.whyNow: 25+ linked customers/SLGs; seen with Quest and LabCorp (large reference labs) — affects many customers.
details.smartToolsFit:
Tools we develop should be a joy to use.
Create tools to improve efficiency of documentation.
details.epicFit:
Solving existing pain points
Software must work, maintain high quality
details.success: Probably not something we'd need to measure.

title: SMARTTOOLS / SPELL CHECK / EXCLUDE PATIENT FIRST AND LAST NAME IN SPELL CHECK

details.problem: As a physician, I get frustrated when my patient's name is flagged as misspelled by the spell checker. This adds extra clicks for me to ensure I'm creating professional documentation.
details.ideaForSolution: Have the spell checker ignore exact matches for the patient's first or last name (except auto-assigned unknown names), and ideally include other personal/care-team names.
details.characteristics: Minimally the patient's name is not marked as misspelled. Stretch: spell checker could suggest minor corrections to names.
details.whyNow: May be worth prioritizing now that Ambient technology and generated transcripts include patient names (not pulled via SmartLink), so spell check will flag names more often.
details.smartToolsFit: Create tools to improve efficiency of documentation.
details.epicFit: Innovate with system intelligence to reduce clinician burnout and improve user efficiency; Software must work.

title: ST - SPELL CHECK - USE VOIKKO FOR FINNISH

details.problem: I am a doctor in Finland. Spell checking is something I expect all programs to have, but our Epic system doesn't have usable Finnish spell checking, making notes look unprofessional.
details.ideaForSolution: Add Voikko to the set of spell check engines available and make it available as an option in the Lexicon Editor (or otherwise support a Finnish-capable spell engine).
details.characteristics: Spell check will work for Finnish, recognizing the majority of words and offering reasonable suggestions.
details.whyNow: Apotti currently disabled Epic's spell check because it was inadequate; better Finnish support would allow re-enabling.
details.smartToolsFit: Software must work (and users should agree that it does).
details.epicFit: Software must work.
details.success: Apotti will re-enable Spell Check and keep it enabled.

title: Add "Expand All" Option for Hover Bubble SmartLinks

details.problem: As a user reviewing other people's notes, I see hover bubble SmartLinks and must hover each to view details; when many items are collapsed, this is time-consuming. I want a quick way to expand all hover bubble SmartLinks in a note (like "expand all" for collapsible sections).
details.ideaForSolution: Make the existing section collapse/expand feature in reports apply to hover bubble SmartLinks, add an "Expand All" option, and ideally remember user preference per EUR node.
details.characteristics: Easy to use without training; leverage existing UI.
details.whyNow: One of the most requested features for hover bubble SmartLinks; required before enabling HBSL by default for some users.
details.epicFit: Solving existing pain points.
details.success: Customer satisfaction improvements per SLG feedback.

title: ST / SmartTextBox / Support webp Image File Format

details.problem: Epic does not support the webp image format; attempting to insert/paste webp images into SmartTextBox fails.
details.ideaForSolution: Let users insert webp files; convert them behind the scenes if necessary.
details.characteristics: It should "just work".
details.whyNow: webp has had wide support since 2018 and is supported by ~97% of browsers; it is becoming ubiquitous.
details.smartToolsFit: Software Must Work > Address compatibility with 3rd party products.
details.epicFit: Innovate and solve existing pain points.
details.success: Pasting/inserting webp images will work.

title: ST / SmartTextBox / Allow setting the \rtlch and \ltrch RTF keywords

details.problem: As a builder, I struggle to create SmartTexts in Arabic and other RTL languages because character-level directionality (\rtlch and \ltrch) is not exposed; mixing English words (addresses/phone numbers) causes punctuation and ordering issues.
details.ideaForSolution: Expose character-level RTL/LTR settings (e.g., toolbar button or paragraph properties) so analysts can explicitly set directionality and reduce copy/paste work.
details.characteristics: Provide character-level RTL/LTR controls, ideally integrated into toolbar and paragraph properties, and explore smarter automatic handling.
details.whyNow: Arabic build is increasingly common; better handling reduces analyst effort.
details.smartToolsFit: SmartTools should be easy to build and expand on.
details.epicFit: Software must work, maintain high quality.
details.success: Analyst happiness.

title: st / pitch / hsweb / smarttextbox - manual jagged table creation

details.problem: Jagged tables (non-rectangular cell layouts) are often necessary to match third-party documents or governmental forms; currently creating them from scratch in the editor is difficult.
details.ideaForSolution: Add tools to create and edit jagged tables: selection-based resizing, linking borders, split/unmerge cells, and more intuitive mouse-based resizing similar to Word.
details.characteristics: We should be able to re-create any jagged table from test documents reliably and without excessive effort.
details.whyNow: Customers frequently need to match 3rd-party documents and label workflows; improves usability and avoids external tools.
details.smartToolsFit: Consistency with Word; improve efficiency and user joy.
details.epicFit: Innovate to reduce clinician burnout and improve user efficiency.
details.success: Ability to recreate jagged tables from sample documents.
details.maintenance: Some added testing for new behaviors.

title: st / pitch / hsweb / smarttextbox - stylesheets

details.problem: Users need consistent document styling across templates; currently they must manually apply formatting which is time-consuming and error-prone.
details.ideaForSolution: Provide style sheet support in SmartTextBox controls (grouped styles like Word), including table styles and customer-customizable style definitions.
details.characteristics: Should feel similar to Word; allow one-click application of styles.
details.whyNow: With more AI-inserted content, users will need easy ways to reformat at scale.
details.smartToolsFit: Consistency with Word; improve efficiency; make it easy to do the right thing.
details.epicFit: Innovate and solve existing pain points.
details.success: User perception that styling feels like Word.
details.maintenance: More overall testing, but grouping existing functionality reduces per-item cost.

title: st / pitch / hsweb / smarttextbox - improved shape support

details.problem: Shapes (positioned shapes) are important for complex documents; Epic's editor has limited create/edit support so users copy/paste from Word and hope it survives.
details.ideaForSolution: Add click-and-drag shape movement, positioning dialog, advanced wrapping styles, editing shape inner content, and the ability to create shapes from scratch.
details.characteristics: Aim to support user needs similar to Word for most vendor documents; focus on commonly encountered documents first.
details.whyNow: Web migration surfaces more documents that rely on shapes; improving shape support reduces reliance on external tools.
details.smartToolsFit: Consistency with Word; increase efficiency and user joy.
details.epicFit: Innovate to reduce clinician burnout and improve user efficiency.
details.success: Support the majority of encountered vendor documents.

title: st / pitch / hsweb / smarttextbox - RTL update and TS migration

details.problem: As a developer, TextLine.js needs conversion from raw JS to TypeScript so RTL-related updates can be implemented safely and maintainably.
details.ideaForSolution: Convert TextLine logic to TypeScript before implementing numerous functional changes to improve type safety and facilitate future RTL work.
details.characteristics: All RTL-related logic in TypeScript with clear structure.
details.whyNow: We are planning RTL changes and it's prudent to align code with the rest of the codebase first.
details.smartToolsFit: Improves developer efficiency and code stability.
details.epicFit: Innovate with system intelligence to improve user/developer efficiency.
details.success: RTL logic fully in TypeScript.

title: ST / Project Pitch / Improve context menu usability

details.problem: As a physician, the SmartTextBox right-click context menu is overwhelming, covers editing areas, and options vary by location; users want a streamlined, personalized menu.
details.ideaForSolution: Implement a system that tracks user habits to tailor context menu options, or otherwise simplify to show the most relevant actions with fewer clicks.
details.characteristics: Show fewer options, reduce submenus, reduce need to dig into deeper menus.
details.whyNow: Saves time and reduces clutter; aligns with simplification goals.
details.epicFit: Solving existing pain points.
details.success: Measure clicks and time users take to reach submenu items.

title: st / pitch / rtf/html - lightweight RTF component for views

details.problem: Developers lack a lightweight, standard component to display RTF-backed content in views without reimplementing RTF-to-HTML logic; current approaches lead to duplicated boilerplate and inconsistent behavior.
details.ideaForSolution: Provide a lightweight RTF display component (between full ReportViewer and raw HTML) that can render RTF as HTML and optionally provide standard features (attribution, low vision).
details.characteristics: Simple rendering by setting a string property; include ReportViewer bells/whistles where feasible.
details.whyNow: Post web transitions, developers need easier ways to consume and display RTF-backed content.
details.smartToolsFit: Consistency across implementations; reduce developer overhead.
details.epicFit: Innovate to reduce developer overhead and frustration.
details.success: Removal of ad-hoc RTF-to-HTML implementations.
details.maintenance: Requires testing and maintenance as a new developer-facing component.

title: ST / SmartTool Editors / Use AI to swap out placeholders for SmartLinks (Tapestry imports, Personalization Station reconciliation)

details.problem: As a SmartTool builder importing third-party templates, I must manually identify varied placeholders and replace them with SmartLinks — tedious and time-consuming.
details.ideaForSolution: Use AI to identify placeholders in RTF/text, replace them with placeholder markup suitable for SmartLinks (maintain RTF formatting), and suggest matching SmartLinks (with confidence scores) or prompt for manual review.
details.characteristics: Confidence scores; preserve RTF formatting; wizard UI for review and replacement; integration with SmartLink lookup.
details.whyNow: Big time-saver for Tapestry and other customers; import volume is high.
details.smartToolsFit: Tools should be easy to build and increase efficiency.
details.epicFit: Solving existing pain points.
details.success: Time saved for builders; reduction in manual editing.

title: ST / Infrastructure / CREATE MASTERFILE FOR SMARTTOOLS CONTEXTS

details.problem: SmartTool contexts are hard to manage: Epic-released SmartLinks may not be available in the desired contexts, builders copy records to maintain availability, and creating new contexts requires updating many SmartLinks.
details.ideaForSolution: Create a new INI/masterfile for SmartTools contexts with hierarchy, allow SmartLinks to inherit contexts, and make editors adapt to context (showing header/footer when editing letter context, etc.).
details.characteristics: Records storing context settings, easier SmartLink maintenance, editor adaptation to context-level settings.
details.whyNow: Saves time and mental load for Epic and customers.

title: ST / Generative AI / Better identify what changed

details.problem: When AI text actions make minimal or moderate edits (spelling, grammar, summaries), it's hard for users to spot what changed in the generated text versus original, increasing the risk of accepting hallucinations or missing omissions.
details.ideaForSolution: Highlight key medical terms, numbers, names, dates, and the exact changed words; provide hover behavior that links original and generated words; or highlight only the changed text for minimal edits and provide an AI banner with Accept All / Reject All.
details.characteristics: Seamless integration with comparison UI; discoverable but not intrusive; facilitates efficient, accurate review.
details.whyNow: Users requested this in demos/usability; improves trustworthy AI usage.

title: ST/ SmartPhrase/ Show user tags in the smarttool butler

details.problem: A clinician has built many tags to organize SmartPhrases in the Manager but can't access or filter by those tags inside the SmartTool butler while editing notes, forcing context switches to the manager.
details.ideaForSolution: Allow filtering by tags in the butler, add a tags column with edit button, and enable tagging for SmartLinks/SmartBlocks so tags are usable in-note.
details.characteristics: Tags usable during authoring, easy to apply, visible, and searchable.
details.whyNow: Tags released Nov 25; users expect integration into in-note workflows.
details.smartToolsFit: Smarttool build and access should reduce time and mental load.
details.epicFit: Help reduce clinician burnout and improve efficiency.
details.success: Users can use tags while writing notes without switching workspaces.

title: ST / Generative AI / Text Actions / Add tagging to custom prompts

details.problem: Once users can write and share custom text prompts, the number of prompts will grow and prompts will be harder to find in the menu.
details.ideaForSolution: Enable tagging for custom prompts (similar to SmartPhrases), submenus/headers in My Prompts, search-by-tag, and distinct UI for shared prompts.
details.characteristics: Custom prompts are easy to find and menu remains clean and simple.
details.whyNow: Sharing prompts is under development and will increase prompt volume.

title: st / rtf/html - field logic should be refactored into derived classes

details.problem: Field.cs logic has grown complex and overloaded with Epic-specific features (citations, TextAction results), making development and testing harder.
details.ideaForSolution: Refactor field logic into a base class with derived classes (like CharacterGroup), moving Epic-specific field types to separate classes for cleaner code and easier maintenance.
details.characteristics: Epic-specific fields in separate classes while still allowing generic processing as needed.
details.whyNow: We are relying more on overloaded fields (AI, citations); refactoring now prevents future complexity.
details.smartToolsFit: Improves efficiency and code stability.
details.success: Known field types represented as separate classes while keeping generic support.

title: DoCo/SmartTools/Create an inline mobile plain text STB control

details.problem: On mobile, editing SmartTextBox fields currently launches a separate view; users want inline editing (SmartList selection and plain text entry) to avoid navigation and extra taps.
details.ideaForSolution: Create an inline mobile plain-text SmartTextBox control for mobile apps that supports inline SmartList selection and reduces jumps into/out of separate activities.
details.characteristics: Seamless inline typing/selection experience, touch-optimized.
details.whyNow: More activities are available on mobile; inline editing would reduce 2+ clicks per field and improve mobile UX.

title: ST / Gen AI / Citation Excerpt Infrastructure

details.problem: When AI SmartLinks produce citations, the hover bubble excerpt may not give enough surrounding context; users must read the whole excerpt to understand the source context.
details.ideaForSolution: Show one line above/below the quote for context and highlight the exact quoted words/phrases with standardized color per UX guide; provide shared RTF markup or API for clients to use consistent citation styles.
details.characteristics: Follow UX guide; consistent cross-system; link RTF markup to HSWeb style colors to avoid hard-coded client colors.
details.whyNow: We already have excerpt highlighting in some released AI citation print groups; as AI SmartLinks expand, consistent excerpt UI will be important.
details.epicFit: Innovate with system intelligence and provide infrastructure for AI features.
details.success: Pair with feature tracking on citation hover reading time (PRJ 345350).

title: ST / May 26 Pitches / AI Text Assistant / Prompts will become harder to find in AITA menu once custom prompts and sharing are enabled

details.problem: With custom prompt creation and sharing, users will have many prompts and the AI Text Assistant menu will become long and hard to navigate.
details.ideaForSolution: Investigate menu structures: semantic/just-in-time surfacing, semantic search, UI redesign (e.g., System Prompts vs My Prompts placement), context-aware suggestions, and other discoverability features.
details.characteristics: "Just in time" prompt discovery; relevant suggestions based on text/workflow context.
details.whyNow: Sharing prompts will increase prompt count and increase menu bloat; avoid poor discoverability.

title: ST / Pitch / The text assistant cannot be used for translation by qualified users

details.problem: Bilingual clinicians cannot use the text assistant to operate on non-English text (e.g., Spanish) even if they are qualified translators.
details.ideaForSolution: Integrate the translation agent and user-language qualification flags (designs already exist) to permit qualified users to use the Text Assistant for translation workflows.
details.characteristics: Secure, respects user qualifications, integrated agent infrastructure, leverages existing translation designs.
details.whyNow: Translation is high-demand; supporting qualified users leverages recent agent/translation design work (Designs 721091, 727660, 730122).

title: ST / Image / Improve performance for image conversion

details.problem: Images take time to convert/render due to server-side image conversion data methods; this causes latency and server load.
details.ideaForSolution: Perform image conversion client-side via WebAssembly or use blob APIs (e.g., fetch TIFF pages as PNG directly) to eliminate server data-method round-trips.
details.characteristics: Eliminate data-method conversion calls, use blob API for TIFF, use WebAssembly for conversions, improve median/p95 render latency, be backward compatible.
details.whyNow: Performance affects every image conversion; reduces server CPU load and operational complexity.
details.smartToolsFit: Push computation to the edge for responsiveness.
details.epicFit: Performance and scalability; cleaner architecture.
details.success: Reduced latency and smoother image interactions.
details.maintenance: Unit and integration tests for single/multi-page and large images.

title: ST / Aug 26 Pitch / Investigate Text Action Template Use Cases

details.problem: Currently templated text actions are only allowed in letters; there may be other useful locations but use cases aren't well-defined.
details.ideaForSolution: Investigate where templated text actions could be useful, assess risk, and provide use case suggestions for broader placement.
details.characteristics: Determine locations and use cases, consider risk and provide recommendations.
details.whyNow: Limited rollout in letters shows value; broader application may increase benefit.
details.smartToolsFit: Make SmartTools easier to discover and use.
details.epicFit: Continue progress on AI Text Assistant.

title: ST / Citation / Make AI citation UI follow the UX guide

details.problem: Different AI features show AI citations inconsistently across apps (e.g., hyperlink placement differs), causing user friction.
details.ideaForSolution: Make AI citation UI consistent and follow the released UX guide.
details.characteristics: Meet the released UX guide.
details.whyNow: Delay increases divergence across systems and harder future overhaul.
details.epicFit: Innovate with infrastructure for system intelligence.
details.success: Conformance to UX guide across features.

title: ST / Aug 26 Pitch / Make the AITA available in more STBs

details.problem: The AI Text Assistant is not available in some SmartTextBoxes (e.g., Custom Prompt editor STB/compact modes), preventing prompt engineering in those contexts.
details.ideaForSolution: Enable AITA in more STB contexts (respect toolbar/compact modes) to match other STB behavior.
details.characteristics: Matches other STB behavior; accessible where editing happens.
details.whyNow: AITA is On By Default for EN-US and NL-NL starting Feb 26.
details.smartToolsFit: Make it easy for users to do the right thing.
details.epicFit: Innovate with system intelligence.
details.success: Users utilize AITA in more STBs.

title: ST / Aug '26 Pitch / Help Me Prompt

details.problem: Physicians don't have time to craft optimal prompts; they need help producing quality prompts quickly.
details.ideaForSolution: Create an agent that evaluates custom prompt quality and suggests improved/alternative prompts.
details.characteristics: Consistently provides adequate prompt alternatives.
details.whyNow: Prompt quality strongly influences AI outcomes and many clinicians won't invest time to learn prompting best practices.
details.epicFit: Innovate with system intelligence to reduce clinician burnout; progress on AI Text Assistant.

title: ST / Generative AI / Make custom prompts smarter

details.problem: Custom prompts only have simplistic input rules (generate at cursor or replace selection) which is limiting and sometimes counter to user intent.
details.ideaForSolution: Use AI to infer input/output behavior from prompt text (e.g., auto-select input, insert at top, display results in banner), provide richer saved-prompt metadata (name/description), and replace the confusing "Requires Selected Text" toggle with AI defaults.
details.characteristics: Matches user expectations, flexible, performant.
details.whyNow: Logical next step as we help users select input automatically.
details.smartToolsFit: Makes AI features powerful and joyful to use.
details.epicFit: Reduce clicks and leverage AI to optimize UX.
details.success: Increased usage of custom prompts.
details.maintenance: More testing for broader prompt behaviors.

title: ST / Navigation / Support one-click navigation

details.problem: Mouse users must click a small button then move in a menu to jump to sections; keyboard users get faster workflows — mouse users could benefit from one-click section navigation.
details.ideaForSolution: Add a navigation bar/top quick-buttons for common headings (e.g., A&P) enabling one-click jumps; recover scrollbar width and be scalable for many headings.
details.characteristics: Intuitive, non-intrusive, scalable.
details.whyNow: Builds on navigation menu project and increases navigation efficiency.
details.smartToolsFit: Make navigating notes a joy.
details.epicFit: Reduce clicks; UX goals.
details.success: Usage of navigation bar; reduced manual scrolling.
details.maintenance: Scalability testing for width/count of headings.

title: ST / Editors / Make it easier to define headings

details.problem: Defining headings requires opening Paragraph Properties per paragraph; headings are invisible without the dialog; no easy way to standardize heading styles across a document.
details.ideaForSolution: Provide a sidebar card to visualize/configure headings, apply heading levels/styles with minimal clicks, auto-detect heading candidates, and provide a Build Wizard to fix existing templates.
details.characteristics: Discoverable, easy to use, intuitive.
details.whyNow: Navigation menu makes heading definition more valuable for usability and accessibility.
details.smartToolsFit: Makes templates accessible and navigable.
details.epicFit: Improve accessibility and navigation.
details.success: Fewer templates without proper headings.
details.maintenance: Add a card and build-wizard testing.

title: ST / Toolbar / Allow configuring more features for one-click access

details.problem: Favorites/pinning still require extra clicks; one-click access requires app buttons or quick buttons which are limited to certain workflows and take UI space.
details.ideaForSolution: Allow configuring quick buttons in any SmartTextBox (customizable, grouped, split-button dropdown) to provide true one-click access to frequently used SmartTools.
details.characteristics: Intuitive, flexible, efficient with minimal UI clutter.
details.whyNow: Simplify project freed toolbar space; natural progression to enable quick buttons there.
details.smartToolsFit: Reduce clicks.
details.epicFit: Make end users more efficient.
details.success: Number of users configuring quick buttons.

title: ST / Personalization Station / Allow uploading and moving user spell check dictionaries

details.problem: When users move institutions, rebuilding personal spell check dictionaries is time-consuming and they often won't, losing productivity.
details.ideaForSolution: Allow users to upload personal lexicons/dictionaries to cloud storage (Epic Earth) to securely transfer between organizations.
details.characteristics: Secure upload/download, integrates with Personalization Station, preserves user dictionaries across orgs.
details.whyNow: Good test ground for adding settings to Personalization Station and improves quality of life.
details.smartToolsFit: Improves provider efficiency and satisfaction.
details.epicFit: Reduce clinician burnout and improve efficiency.
details.success: Feature tracking for uploads to Epic Earth.
details.maintenance: Storage considerations; testing via internal tools.

title: ST / SmartLink Editor / Make it easy to compare and contrast similar SmartLinks

details.problem: Analysts have difficulty knowing whether a similar SmartLink already exists; end users struggle to pick the right SmartLink when many look similar.
details.ideaForSolution: Provide tools to find similar SmartLinks (by code template/description), show side-by-side comparisons, and help organize variants.
details.characteristics: Intuitive comparison view; easy to distinguish and choose the right SmartLink.
details.whyNow: Consistent feedback from analysts and users; reduces bloat and duplication.
details.smartToolsFit: Make it easy to build and find the right SmartTools.
details.epicFit: Solving existing pain points.
details.success: Qualitative feedback and reduced duplication.

title: ST / Butler / Address quality of life issues in the SmartTool butler

details.problem: Finding SmartLinks/SmartPhrases in the butler is hard (can't search body text, tags/filters missing), causing friction when improving templates.
details.ideaForSolution: Add technical notes, saved filters, better "full text" descriptions, categories/tags, grouping by code template, and ability to see other users’ SmartPhrases from the butler.
details.characteristics: Combination of enhancements to improve searching, sorting, and discovering SmartTools.
details.whyNow: Butler is widely used; small QoL improvements empower many users.
details.smartToolsFit: Reduce time and mental load to find SmartTools.
details.epicFit: Solving existing pain points.
details.success: Microfeature tracking and qualitative feedback.

title: ST / SmartTextBox / Consolidate SmartTool search buttons into one

details.problem: Separating SmartPhrases, SmartTexts, and the butler into multiple buttons is confusing and clutters the toolbar.
details.ideaForSolution: Consolidate the butler, SmartText, and SmartList fields into a single searchable field to simplify discoverability and reduce toolbar clutter.
details.characteristics: Consistent, simple SmartTool discovery without remembering types.
details.whyNow: Aligns with Simplify Rich Text initiatives and reduces mental load.
details.smartToolsFit: Reduce mental load to find SmartTools.
details.epicFit: Solving existing pain points observed during immersions.
details.success: Usage metrics and reduced toolbar clutter score.

title: ST / SmartLink Editor / Auto populate descriptions and categories

details.problem: SmartLink descriptions are often missing or insufficient, making discovery hard.
details.ideaForSolution: Automatically generate useful descriptions and category metadata for new or sparse SmartLinks (AI-generated) to aid search and maintenance.
details.characteristics: Automatically generated descriptions that improve search/discovery and support future AI tooling.
details.whyNow: Helps analysts and end users find the correct SmartLinks and lays groundwork for AI tooling.
details.smartToolsFit: Make it easy to find and maintain SmartTools.
details.epicFit: Enhance pain points.
details.success: Measure discoveries/follow-up usage where descriptions were generated; qualitative analysis of description quality.
details.maintenance: AI costs for generation; quality checks.

title: ST / Custom Prompts / Create a Manager activity similar to the SmartPhrase Manager

details.problem: Analysts maintain custom prompts in disparate ways and lack a centralized activity to view, filter, search, and manage organization-level prompts.
details.ideaForSolution: Build a Custom Prompt Manager activity similar to SmartPhrase Manager with filtering by level (DEP/system), deletion, and full-text search.
details.characteristics: List org prompts, filter by level, delete unused prompts, search within prompts.
details.whyNow: Sharing custom prompts will make prompt maintenance a pain point once released.
details.epicFit: AI initiative.

title: ST / Butler / Promote results that are commonly used by similar users

details.problem: Searching the butler is hard; many similar SmartTools make it difficult to find the best one.
details.ideaForSolution: Promote search results in the butler that are highly used by similar users (role/department), surfacing likely appropriate tools.
details.characteristics: Results with high usage among similar users appear at top and are highlighted without hiding other results.
details.whyNow: Many similar SmartTools exist and role/peer-based promotion can guide users toward tested items.
details.epicFit: Enhancements for pain points.
details.success: Track promoted-result display vs usage and adoption.

title: ST / Text Actions / Add in user to user sharing

details.problem: Creators of custom Text Actions currently cannot easily share them with specific users or groups.
details.ideaForSolution: Allow sharing custom Text Actions to single users or groups, add search for existing shared actions, and create security for editing/sharing.
details.characteristics: Searchable, share-to-user/group, security for edit/share, discoverable for recipients.
details.whyNow: After moving custom text actions to records, sharing is a high-priority enhancement.

title: st / pitch / hsweb / smarttextbox - inline header/footer support

details.problem: Managing header/footer content for complex templates (letters) is cumbersome because they're often stored separately; authors need to edit and view the whole document inline.
details.ideaForSolution: Support inline header/footer editing and rendering, echo content to linked records, or use SmartSections to represent multi-part documents while keeping consumers stable.
details.characteristics: View and edit up-to-date header/footer inline; support swapping and sharing header/footer definitions across templates.
details.whyNow: Tapestry and other projects illustrate need; improves fidelity for governmental/third-party print requirements.
details.smartToolsFit: Consistency with Word; better editor experience.
details.epicFit: Reduce clinician/analyst pain and make templates more reliable.
details.success: Correct rendering for both historic and new header/footer storage methods.
details.maintenance: Some additional maintenance/testing to cover both storage models.

title: ST/ Personalization Station/ Reconciling SmartLists by tracking the original SmartList data and mapping to existing records on the receiving side

details.problem: When users move SmartPhrases/SmartLists via Personalization Station, the receiving system often can't map SmartLists and replaces them with placeholders, forcing manual reconciliation.
details.ideaForSolution: Include source SmartList metadata (text, selections, nested items) in the uploaded Turbocharger schema; on download, attempt automatic mapping to local SmartLists, suggest matches in a reconciliation UI, or create user SmartLists when appropriate.
details.characteristics: Embed SmartList info in import data, try automatic mapping, provide reconciliation UI, avoid mapping SmartLists with SDEs automatically.
details.whyNow: Makes Personalization Station more useful and reduces manual effort for customers.
details.smartToolsFit: Tools should be easy to build and transfer.
details.epicFit: Solving existing pain points and improving migration experience.
details.success: User feedback and fewer manual reconciliations.
details.maintenance: Some mapping logic and reconciliation UI testing.

title: Create an easier way to have "vanishing tips" (aka help text)

details.problem: As a physician builder for my specialty, I want to provide hints, tips, or other types of information that show up in a note when it is being edited, but disappear when the note is signed to not clutter the note. I can use an optional SmartList, but there are a number of issues with this: The SmartLinks I want to pull in sometimes include colons in the text which prevents signing the note. My analysts tell me there are issues with my build when they try to move it to production using Data Courier when I use a colon in the text where Data Courier finds the wrong SmartList or doesn't update the optional SmartList's record ID correctly.
details.ideaForSolution: If Epic created a new way to adding disappearing help text to a note that would allow me, and the analysts that help me, be more efficient.
details.characteristics: SmartSection!

title: ST / Pitch / AI Text Assistant can misunderstand abbreviations

details.problem: Clinicians using the AI Text Assistant on documents that contain abbreviations may get surprising and incorrect results. Many abbreviations are locale specific, department specific, role specific, or ambiguous. Today, the text assistant has no additional context for understanding these abbreviations. For common abbreviations, this works well, but for non obvious abbreviations, the text assistant will not handle this well.
details.ideaForSolution:
I have yet to find a clear and easy solution, but here's what we've explored so far:

The general idea of this flow is to create a large list of abbreviations + definitions. Then, when we string match on input text and find these abbreviations, we append to the bottom of the prompt the definition(s) of these abbreviation(s). From testing that LJ did, this works very well when one of the abbreviation definitions is correct, even if there are multiple options for what the abbreviation might mean. However, this approach is slightly worse than no context if we supply only incorrect definitions for these abbreviation. Creating a robust definition list is a critical pre-req for this to work.

We could do this with some combination of:
1. Create a locale specific default list of abbreviations + definitions. Allow TS to customize these abbreviations for their organization. Likely allow department and maybe even role specific default list changes as well.
2. Build off of user dictionaries to allow user customization for abbreviations they use in their practice regularly. Note that this would require changes to user dictionaries since today, we allow people to mark something as not a misspelling but don't require them to define it.

Potentially, we could build this user dictionary up over time and maybe find an intelligent way to ask what an abbreviation means, such as when a user goes to reject a text assistant response that involved an abbreviation.
details.characteristics:
Scaleable to other AI features. This is a problem that nearly all AI features currently have and none have solved.
Maintainable. This doesn't work if this becomes a huge burden for TS and customers to maintain.
Easy to setup. This doesn't work if the initial lift to get this setup is a huge burden, especially for small customers with few IT resources.
Ideally this helps to build trust as the system "learns" as the user interacts with AI features.
details.whyNow:
This has been a known and longstanding issue so it is unclear if we need to solve this now. There are some customers in the Netherlands who routinely ask about this problem.

I think there is a chance that this blows up at some point though and we need to move quickly on this. Right now, we could start an investigation into options and get ahead of any potential escalation.

If we do this well, this has a chance to differentiate Epic AI features from 3rd parties since we could be more intelligent and customized to how healthcare is practiced in a customer's local region. For users, it could build trust that the system understands how they practice healthcare. It's a great opportunity to localize AI tools and would help as AI adoption in international locales increases.
details.maintenance: Potentially high, this needs to be factored into design options.

title: ST / Feb '26 Pitch / The text assistant is not context aware

details.problem: When a clinician uses the AI text assistant, it will give responses that clearly do not pay attention to the surrounding context. This means responses are less useful than they could be and it makes the tool less useful and usable. An example is in In Basket, where the model doesn't understand most information about the patient or what the last patient message was, making it difficult for it to suggest useful responses to the patient.
details.ideaForSolution: Build a generic infrastructure that allows app team devs to pass relevant context for their workflows to the AI Text Assistant. For IB, this could look like devs passing IB Insights and the last patient message. For OP Notes, this could look like passing the patient chart summary. For IP and ED notes, this could look like passing IP and ED insights. Clinicians using the text assistant would then likely get far better and more tailored responses.
details.whyNow: This gives us a chance to stand on our hill of having a deeply integrated system. No other AI editing tool can integrate with the rest of the patient's chart in the way we can. This is a clear differentiator and potentially makes using the text assistant consistently more useful than going to ChatGPT, not just more convenient and safer.
details.epicFit: System intelligence improvements.
details.success: Consider feature tracking logging once for each context aware text action that is available and another for each time it gets used. Consider logging / reporting for the individual text action for more granular data.

title: ST / Text Assistant / Let Users Supply Custom Context

details.problem: As a clinician, I want the ability to add my own context to the AI Text Assistant, such as replacing "hello" with "aloha", making it sound more like me by supplying a writing sample, or letting it reference 3rd party clinical documentation that I use for patient care. This will help reduce my cognitive burden and focus more on patient care.
details.ideaForSolution: Now, the AI Text Assistant has an even better understanding of my tone and makes the note sound more like me.
details.characteristics:
Provide users (or organizations) a way to:
1. Supply custom instructions to include as part of the prompt infrastructure
2. Reference 3rd party documentation, such as clinical references, to include in their notes
3. Create a flexible framework to expand on new use cases
details.whyNow: Give users the best experience with AI. AITA is one of the ST cornerstone AI features.
details.smartToolsFit: Reduce cognitive burden and duplicative documentation; make the text assistant feel more personal; make it more fun to use
details.epicFit: This is (possibly) the next step of forager.
details.success: Increased adoption and widespread use of the AI Text Assistant. Track the amount and types of custom context added by users. This can also help inform future out-of-the-box text actions.
details.maintenance: No/low. Users would store their own context and/or opt into the behavior.

title: ST / Generative AI / Text Actions / Check for logical contradictions

details.problem: From talking to various customers, there can be logical contradictions in what gets documented in the patient's chart. As one example, a user copies forward a note that says "all lab results normal" but then adds in documentation for a new abnormal lab result. These types of contradictions have also been observed in ambient generated notes coming from DAX.
details.ideaForSolution:
We develop a new prompt that can take in text and return any logical contradictions that exist in that text.

This can then be plugged into other AI workflows, such as native ambient. Native ambient devs have already expressed interest in building on this idea.

We could also consider releasing this as a prompt in the text assistant. Users could then highlight a note, right click, run this prompt, and see any contradictions in it displayed at the bottom.

We could look at integrating this type of contradiction check into the background, similar to ideas we're exploring for catching stigmatizing language in the background.
